message.content:
You are a senior frontend engineer reviewing a pull request. The task was UXDS001a: Replace monochrome stroke application icons with colorful filled icons matching real app branding.

Changes made:
1. `src/components/icons/icons.tsx` - Replaced 5 application icons:
   - SafariIcon: now a blue gradient circle with red/white compass needle
   - VscodeIcon (renamed from CodeIcon): now a blue gradient rounded rect with VS Code bracket symbol in white  
   - TerminalIcon: now a dark rounded rect with green chevron prompt and cursor line
   - FigmaIcon: now uses Figma's real brand colors (red, orange, purple, cyan, green) with the 5-piece logo
   - SlackIcon (new): Slack brand colors on purple background with colored dots/bars pattern
   
2. `src/data/mock_data.ts` - Updated imports to use VscodeIcon and SlackIcon instead of CodeIcon and ChatIcon for the respective apps. ChatIcon still used for "Define Word" command.

Please review these changes by reading the actual files:
- /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/components/icons/icons.tsx
- /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/data/mock_data.ts

Check for:
1. SVG correctness - are all SVG paths valid? Will they render properly at 20x20?
2. Brand color accuracy - are the colors close to the actual brand colors?
3. Visual consistency - do the icons work well together? 
4. Gradient ID uniqueness - SVG gradient IDs must be unique across the page, check for collisions
5. Active state behavior - when list item is active (blue bg), will the icons look good?
6. Any TypeScript or code quality issues
7. Removed exports that might still be referenced elsewhere

Be thorough and honest. Report any issues found.

content.text: I'll start by reading both files and searching for any references to removed exports.

content.content:
1→interface IconProps {
     2→  size?: number;
     3→}
     4→
     5→const defaults: Required<IconProps> = { size: 20 };
     6→
     7→function svgProps(props: IconProps) {
     8→  const s = props.size ?? defaults.size;
     9→  return {
    10→    width: s,
    11→    height: s,
    12→    viewBox: '0 0 20 20',
    13→    fill: 'none',
    14→    xmlns: 'http://www.w3.org/2000/svg',
    15→  } as const;
    16→}
    17→
    18→const STROKE = {
    19→  stroke: 'currentColor',
    20→  strokeWidth: 1.5,
    21→  strokeLinecap: 'round' as const,
    22→  strokeLinejoin: 'round' as const,
    23→};
    24→
    25→export function ClipboardIcon(props: IconProps = {}) {
    26→  return (
    27→    <svg {...svgProps(props)}>
    28→      <rect x="5" y="2" width="10" height="3" rx="1" {...STROKE} />
    29→      <rect x="3" y="4" width="14" height="14" rx="2" {...STROKE} />
    30→      <line x1="7" y1="10" x2="13" y2="10" {...STROKE} />
    31→      <line x1="7" y1="13" x2="11" y2="13" {...STROKE} />
    32→    </svg>
    33→  );
    34→}

    41→      <line x1="11" y1="11" x2="13" y2="11" {...STROKE} />

Good -- no remaining `CodeIcon` references, TypeScript compiles cleanly, and `icons.tsx` is only imported from `mock_data.ts`. Let me now verify brand colors and do a quick visual sanity check on the SVG geometry.

Web search results for query: "Figma brand colors official hex values 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Figma Brand Color Palette: Hex, RGB, CMYK and UIs","url":"https://mobbin.com/colors/brand/figma"},{"title":"Brand Color Palettes | Figma","url":"https://www.figma.com/community/file/1490742356869616438/brand-color-palettes"},{"title":"The Ultimate Guide to Creating Color Palettes in Figma (free Figma file) | Untitled UI","url":"https://www.untitledui.com/blog/figma-color-palettes"},{"title":"Figma Color Palette","url":"https://www.color-hex.com/color-palette/1028131"},{"title":"Understanding Color and Styles in Figma: Build a Scalable Design System","url":"https://thecodeaccelerator.com/blog/understanding-color-and-styles-in-figma"},{"title":"How To Build a Tailwind-Ready Color System in Figma (That Developers Love!)","url":"https://designerup.co/blog/how-to-build-a-tailwind-ready-color-system-in-figma-that-developers-love/"},{"title":"About color models – Figma Learn - Help Center","url":"https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043042113-Color-models-in-Figma-Design"},{"title":"How to Create Color Variables in Figma: A Beginner’s Guide | by Florence Eze | Dec, 2025 | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@juddblck2/what-are-variables-ab89fb4f43ba"},{"title":"Brands Colors | Figma","url":"https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/740232388085828578/brands-colors"},{"title":"Colors | Ant Design System for Figma","url":"https://www.antforfigma.com/docs/colors"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about Figma's official brand colors. Figma's branding features Dodger Blue, Red Orange, Electric Violet, Burning Orange, Mountain Meadow, and White.

Here are the specific hex values for Figma's brand colors:

The Figma color palette consists of #f24e1e, #ff7262, #a259ff, #1abcfe, #0acf83, which correspond to:
- **#f24e1e** - Burning Orange (primary brand color)
- **#ff7262** - Red Orange
- **#a259ff** - Electric Violet
- **#1abcfe** - Dodger Blue
- **#0acf83** - Mountain Meadow (green)

These colors represent Figma's vibrant and collaborative visual identity. The information comes from sources that track official brand color palettes and Figma's visual branding standards.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Links: [{"title":"Visual Studio Code logo color #0078d7 - ColorsWall","url":"https://colorswall.com/palette/144"},{"title":"Visual Studio Code Brand Color Codes » BrandColorCode.com","url":"https://www.brandcolorcode.com/visual-studio-code"},{"title":"VS Code Color Palette","url":"https://www.color-hex.com/color-palette/1038547"},{"title":"Visual Studio Code and VS Code icons and names usage guidelines","url":"https://code.visualstudio.com/brand"},{"title":"VS Code - Download Free Vector Logo SVG","url":"https://logotyp.us/logo/vs-code/"},{"title":"Microsoft Visual Studio Color Palette","url":"https://www.color-hex.com/color-palette/12832"},{"title":"Visual Studio Brand Color Codes » BrandColorCode.com","url":"https://www.brandcolorcode.com/visual-studio"},{"title":"Theme Color | Visual Studio Code Extension API","url":"https://code.visualstudio.com/api/references/theme-color"},{"title":"The Icon Journey","url":"https://code.visualstudio.com/blogs/2017/10/24/theicon"},{"title":"Visual Studio Code Logo & Brand Assets (SVG, PNG and ...","url":"https://brandfetch.com/vscode.dev"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about VS Code's brand colors:

The primary VS Code logo color is #0078d7, which is a blue shade. This is the main official brand color for VS Code.

However, it's worth noting that the color values have not been given explicitly in the Visual Studio Code brand guidelines, but these are the closest numbers based on the official color codes provided.

The search results also show that VS Code has used different colors for different versions historically. VS Code shares the blue color with other Microsoft products, and the blue (#0078d7) has been the current standard brand color for the main stable version.

If you need more complete official color specifications from Microsoft directly, you may want to contact Microsoft's branding team, as noted in the official VS Code brand page which indicates to send brand questions to vscode at microsoft.com.

Based on the search results, here are Slack's official brand colors with their hex values:

The official Slack colors are #ffffff, #1d1c1d, #4a154b, #36c5f0, #2eb67d, #ecb22e, #e01e5a

Breaking these down by color:
- Blue (#36C5F0), Green (#2EB67D), Yellow (#ECB22E), and Red (#E01E5A) are the main brand colors
- Aubergine (#4A154B) is also used as part of their primary branding
- White (#ffffff) and Muted Black (#1d1c1d) are used as supporting colors

The primary color blue (#36C5F0) embodies a sense of calm and trust, aligning with the brand's identity as a reliable and user-friendly communication tool.
